questions.q: In which field of Indian cinema was S. D. Burman a celebrated figure?
questions.explanation: S. D. Burman was one of Hindi cinema's most renowned music directors, active from the 1940s to the 1970s.

questions.q: Music director S. D. Burman was the father of which famous Hindi film composer?
questions.explanation: S. D. Burman was the father and frequent collaborator of composer R. D. Burman.

questions.q: The regional folk traditions of which Indian region were a major influence on S. D. Burman's music?
questions.explanation: S. D. Burman fused Bengali folk traditions with Hindustani classical music in his film scores.

questions.q: S. D. Burman composed the music for which acclaimed 1957 Guru Dutt film?
questions.explanation: S. D. Burman scored Guru Dutt's 1957 classic Pyaasa.

questions.q: For which 1965 Dev Anand film did S. D. Burman compose the celebrated music?
questions.explanation: S. D. Burman composed the famous score of Guide (1965), starring Dev Anand and Waheeda Rehman.

questions.q: S. D. Burman scored which 1969 Rajesh Khanna film featuring the song 'Roop Tera Mastana'?
questions.explanation: S. D. Burman composed the music for Aradhana (1969), one of the films that launched Rajesh Khanna's superstardom.

questions.q: In which decade did S. D. Burman begin his career as a music director in Hindi cinema?
questions.explanation: S. D. Burman began scoring Hindi films in the 1940s, working through to the 1970s.

questions.q: Apart from composing, S. D. Burman was also known for occasionally doing what on his own film tracks?
questions.explanation: S. D. Burman was also a singer, lending his distinctive folk-tinged voice to several of his own compositions.

questions.q: S. D. Burman frequently collaborated with which actor who was also a producer of films like Guide and Jewel Thief?
questions.explanation: S. D. Burman had a long and fruitful association with Dev Anand and his Navketan banner.

questions.q: S. D. Burman belonged to which princely lineage of his home region?
questions.explanation: S. D. Burman was born into the royal family of Tripura.

questions.q: In which year was S. D. Burman born?
questions.explanation: Sachin Dev Burman was born in 1906.

questions.q: In which year did S. D. Burman die?
questions.explanation: S. D. Burman died in 1975.

questions.q: What did the initials 'S. D.' in S. D. Burman's name stand for?
questions.explanation: S. D. Burman's full name was Sachin Dev Burman.

questions.q: Which lyricist wrote the songs of the 1965 S. D. Burman-scored film Guide, including 'Gaata Rahe Mera Dil'?
questions.explanation: Shailendra wrote the lyrics for Guide (1965), set to S. D. Burman's music, including songs such as 'Gaata Rahe Mera Dil'.

questions.q: Which lyricist wrote many of S. D. Burman's songs in the 1950s before a falling-out, including for Pyaasa?
questions.explanation: Sahir Ludhianvi collaborated extensively with S. D. Burman in the 1950s, including on Pyaasa, before they parted ways.

questions.q: S. D. Burman composed the music for which 1959 Guru Dutt film about a fading film director?
questions.explanation: S. D. Burman scored Guru Dutt's 1959 film Kaagaz Ke Phool, which dealt with a fading film director.

questions.q: S. D. Burman provided music for which 1958 thriller featuring the song 'Hai Apna Dil To Awara'?
questions.explanation: 'Hai Apna Dil To Awara', composed by S. D. Burman, featured in the 1958 film Solva Saal.

questions.q: Which female playback singer's voice was prominently used by S. D. Burman across many of his films, including Aradhana?
questions.explanation: S. D. Burman worked extensively with Lata Mangeshkar, including on Aradhana, despite a temporary rift in the 1950s.

questions.q: S. D. Burman composed the music for which 1954 Dev Anand crime film set partly around taxi drivers?
questions.explanation: S. D. Burman scored the 1954 Navketan film Taxi Driver, starring Dev Anand.

questions.q: The 1951 Dev Anand film Baazi, an early success for S. D. Burman, was directed by whom?
questions.explanation: Guru Dutt made his directorial debut with Baazi (1951), scored by S. D. Burman.

questions.q: S. D. Burman composed for which 1967 Dev Anand spy thriller directed by Vijay Anand?
questions.explanation: S. D. Burman scored Jewel Thief (1967), directed by Vijay Anand and starring Dev Anand.

questions.q: S. D. Burman's son R. D. Burman is widely known by which affectionate nickname?
questions.explanation: R. D. Burman, S. D. Burman's son, was popularly known as 'Pancham'.

questions.q: S. D. Burman won the Filmfare Award for Best Music Director for which 1973 film?
questions.explanation: S. D. Burman won the Filmfare Best Music Director award for Abhimaan (1973).

questions.q: S. D. Burman composed the songs for which 1975 Hrishikesh Mukherjee comedy starring Dharmendra and Amitabh Bachchan?
questions.explanation: S. D. Burman scored Hrishikesh Mukherjee's comedy Chupke Chupke (1975), one of his late works alongside Abhimaan (1973) and Mili (1975).

questions.q: In which Indian city did S. D. Burman work as a radio singer and build his early reputation before moving into films?
questions.explanation: S. D. Burman established his early career as a singer in Calcutta before moving to Bombay for films.

questions.q: S. D. Burman composed the music for which 1963 Bimal Roy film starring Nutan as a woman imprisoned for a crime of passion?
questions.explanation: S. D. Burman scored Bimal Roy's Bandini (1963), known for songs like 'Mora Gora Ang Laile' and 'O Re Maajhi'.

questions.q: The song 'Mora Gora Ang Laile' from Bandini, composed by S. D. Burman, marked the film debut as lyricist of whom?
questions.explanation: Gulzar made his lyricist debut with 'Mora Gora Ang Laile' in Bandini, set to S. D. Burman's music.

questions.q: S. D. Burman scored which 1958 film starring Kishore Kumar that featured the song 'Ek Ladki Bheegi Bhaagi Si'?
questions.explanation: S. D. Burman composed for Chalti Ka Naam Gaadi (1958), which featured 'Ek Ladki Bheegi Bhaagi Si' sung by Kishore Kumar.

questions.q: Which classical instrument did S. D. Burman notably weave into film music, reflecting his folk and classical training?
questions.explanation: S. D. Burman often used the flute and folk-rooted melodies, evoking the riverine Bengali soundscape of his roots.

questions.q: S. D. Burman composed the music for which final film of his that was released around the time of his death in 1975?
questions.explanation: Mili (1975) was among S. D. Burman's last completed works, released around the time of his death.
